root.narration: You are a center-back and the goalkeeper just passed you the ball. The other team is pressing with two forwards. Your midfielders are being marked but your other center-back is open. Your right-back has dropped deep to give you an option wide. How do you play out?

choices.text: Play it wide to your right-back who is open and has space to carry the ball forward!
Pass to your center-back partner to shift the pressing forwards and create a new angle.
Kick it long up the field to get rid of the pressure!

outcome.headline: Played Through the Press!
outcome.explanation: Your right-back was in space and the press could not reach them. They received the ball, turned forward, and carried it into midfield -- you have bypassed the entire press! Playing wide to a fullback is often the key to beating a high press because the wide areas are usually where the gaps appear.
outcome.whatToRemember: When being pressed, look for fullbacks in wide positions. The press usually leaves space on the outside! Play wide to escape the pressure.

keyTerms.definition: Building your attack with short passes from the goalkeeper and defenders instead of kicking it long.
Using passing and positioning to get past a team that is pressing you aggressively.

outcome.headline: Shifted the Press, But Still Stuck!
outcome.explanation: Passing to your center-back partner moved the two forwards and bought time. That is a good first step! But the press just shifted with the ball. The key is to use that first pass to create a better angle for the NEXT pass -- ideally to a fullback or a midfielder who drops into space. Two passes beats a press better than one.
outcome.whatToRemember: Passing across the back line shifts the press and creates new angles. Use it as a stepping stone to find the real escape route!

outcome.headline: Long Ball Lost! They Have It Back!
outcome.explanation: Booting it long gave up possession. The other team's press worked -- they forced you into a panicked long ball. This is exactly what pressing teams WANT you to do. Playing out of the back takes bravery and trust in your teammates. Your right-back was open and ready. Next time, play the calm, short pass to keep the ball.
outcome.whatToRemember: Do not panic and boot it long when being pressed! Stay calm, trust your teammates, and find the free player. Long balls give the press what it wants.
